Off Calendar Mar-10-2010 Granted Per Written Order

Successor Administrator Christine Delaneys Report Of Status Of Administration
Off Calendar Feb-03-2010 Continued To Mar-10-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Frederick James for Christine Delaney.
Mr. James stated that he did respond to the Examiner's notes from December, that he had requested additional time, that the Administrator sent the bank statements, that the amount on hand stated was the actual amount from the previous year, that the cash on hand amount is $1,000.00, and that there was some additions for interest and some subtractions for fees. In addition, he stated that he had not filed the supplemental declaration, that the bank account was earning interest, that there was a major issue with the closing of the estate due to the outstanding judgment entered against Mr. Simpson on February 23, 2000, that he had submitted the request to renew the judgment, that the judgment was probably not collectable, that he had collected on the 1/3 interest of a property Mr. Simpson owned in San Francisco, and that a search on Mr. Simpson did not show him owning any other property. Furthermore, he stated that the 5 year holding period for the amount held for the unlocated Beneficiary (Child of Leo Sayles) was up on November 2, 2010, that he was undecided as to the manner in which he would collect on the judgment, that Mr. Simpson testified that his only source income was social security and that $50,000.00 due to Ms. Deborah Brooks had not been paid as it was to come out of the money collected from the judgment against Mr. Simpson. The Court directed Mr. James to file a supplemental declaration addressing all of the issues raised in the Examiner's notes as well as all of the issued raised by the Court by close of business day Tuesday February 16, 2010. a.m., in Dept. 204. Status of administration was continued to March 10, 2010 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Status of filing of the status report was set for November 24, 2010. Status of closing of the estate was set for December 28, 2010.
Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera.
Bailiff: Deputy J. Capozzoli.
